titleStmt.title: Letter from Robert King Irvine to Edward O'Farrell, 31 May 1916
Letters 1916-1923
titleStmt.author: Robert King Irvine

editionStmt.p: This work was originally published by Maynooth University in Ireland in 2017. In 2026 this data, stored in a relational database was extracted and converted into this TEI/XML document.

availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

note.p: This is a letter from Robert King Irvine (b.1877), a company owner and director, to Edward O'Farrell (1857-1926), the assistant under-secretary for Ireland. The letter is written over a month after the 1916 Rising. It asks what measures are being taken to rid Dublin Castle of 'Sinn Feiners', the more extreme nationalists associated with participation in the Easter Rising.

msIdentifier.collection: Chief Secretary's Office Registered Papers, CSO RP/1916/5612/9395

projectDesc.p: The data in these XML files was generated based on a data dump from the Magellan database (https://github.com/Maynooth-Center-for-Digital-Humanities/Magellan). Each database record has been converted to a JSON file (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/json). The element section of the JSON file contains the TEI-encoded contents of the letters. The TEI XML has been cleaned and made well-formed using a Python script (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/src).

body.ab:
Chamber of Commerce Dublin 31st of May 1916 Sir, Could you kindly give me
any information as to what
steps, if any, are being
taken to purge Irish
Government Offices of
Sinn Feiners. I am Sir
Yours faithfully
R King Irvine Sir Edward O'Farrell Dublin Castle Ackn & reply
EOF
1 VI -16
